author: OpenList Team
tagline: Mount your cloud drive on your home NAS
description:
OpenList is a community-driven, self-hosted file listing and cloud drive mounting service that supports multiple storage backends in one place.
It brings local storage plus services like OneDrive, Google Drive, S3, WebDAV, SMB, and many other providers into one browser-based file hub.
With previews, uploads, sharing, WebDAV, offline download, and package download built in, it works well for home NAS setups, personal cloud libraries, and lightweight team file portals.

**Main Features:**
- Connect local disks, cloud drives, object storage, and WebDAV or SMB endpoints in one place
- Preview documents, images, audio, video, code, Markdown, and Office files directly in the browser
- Upload, move, rename, copy, delete, and batch download files and folders from a single interface
- Enable WebDAV, protected routes, sharing, dark mode, and multi-language support
- Support offline downloads, cross-storage file copying, and multi-thread acceleration

**Learn More:**
- [OpenList Official Website](https://oplist.org/)
- [OpenList GitHub](https://github.com/OpenListTeam/OpenList)

tips.before_install:
Default username: `admin`
Default password: `casaos`
If you want to change the password, please modify it in the OpenList WebUI.

release_notes:
- v4.2.2 includes breaking cache changes by extracting the hybrid_cache package.
- Includes upstream fixes and maintenance from the 4.2.2 release.
- Review the upstream release notes before upgrading existing custom cache integrations.
title: OpenList
